content.s1_claims_discipline_internal:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TAGS: pregnancy, prenatal, fertility, preconception, postpartum, breastfeeding, IVF, egg quality, sperm quality, folate, folic acid, DHA pregnancy, iron pregnancy, morning sickness, gestational diabetes, preeclampsia, miscarriage, trimester, prenatal vitamins, fertility supplements, conceive, trying to conceive, TTC, ovulation, implantation, pregnant, postnatal, conception, lactation, pregnancy supplements, folate pregnancy, pregnancy safe, pregnancy IV, omega-3 pregnancy, vitamin D pregnancy, iodine pregnancy, choline pregnancy, calcium pregnancy, zinc pregnancy, magnesium pregnancy, nausea vomiting NVP, hyperemesis gravidarum, PCOS fertility, endometriosis fertility, thyroid pregnancy TSH, iron pregnancy anemia, prenatal protocol
# CATEGORY: Special Populations
# USE FOR: Pregnancy support, prenatal nutrition, fertility optimization, postpartum recovery, pregnancy-safe treatments
# COUPLED_SYSTEMS: 18_FEMALE_HORMONES, 17_MALE_HORMONES, 04_NUTRITION_DIET, 54_DRUG_INTERACTIONS, 00_CRITICAL_REFERENCE
# RED_FLAGS: Bleeding in pregnancy, severe headache + vision changes (preeclampsia), severe abdominal pain, reduced fetal movement, fever in pregnancy
# TRIAGE_QUESTIONS: Currently pregnant (which trimester) or trying to conceive? Any complications? On any medications?
# IV_ROLE: IV therapy during pregnancy requires PHYSICIAN APPROVAL for every session. Basic hydration IV is generally safe. NAD+ IV is NOT recommended during pregnancy (insufficient safety data). High-dose vitamin C IV is NOT recommended during pregnancy. Glutathione IV — insufficient data. Default to oral supplementation (prenatal vitamins, iron, folate, magnesium).
# INTERVENTION_PRIORITY: Prenatal nutrition → Oral supplements (prenatal, iron, folate) → IV hydration ONLY with physician approval → NAD+/high-dose C NOT recommended in pregnancy
# TELECONSULT_LIMITS: CAN do remotely: general nutrition guidance, safe supplement advice, prenatal wellness support. CANNOT do remotely: any medical decision without OB/midwife coordination, ultrasound, physical exam, supplement changes without physician approval. CRITICAL: pregnancy requires extreme caution remotely. Default to 'discuss with your OB' for any clinical decision.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PREGNANCY & PRECONCEPTION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 === SECTION 1: CLAIMS DISCIPLINE [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## IMPORTANT DISCLAIMER

**Always work with your OB-GYN or midwife during pregnancy.**

- Inform them of all supplements
- Some supplements are contraindicated in pregnancy
- Dosing changes during pregnancy
- Individual circumstances vary
- This is supportive information, not medical advice

---

## PRECONCEPTION: PREPARING FOR PREGNANCY

**Ideal timeline:** Begin preparation 3-6 months before trying to conceive.

Why preparation matters:
- Egg quality takes ~3 months to develop
- Sperm takes ~3 months to mature
- Nutrient stores need time to build
- Toxin reduction takes time
- Optimizing health before pregnancy = Better outcomes

### Preconception Checklist

**Both Partners:**
- [ ] Optimize nutrient status
- [ ] Address any chronic conditions
- [ ] Reduce toxin exposure
- [ ] Optimize weight (if needed)
- [ ] Reduce alcohol and caffeine
- [ ] Stop smoking
- [ ] Manage stress
- [ ] Review medications with doctor
- [ ] Dental checkup (treat any issues before pregnancy)

**Women:**
- [ ] Start prenatal vitamins (3+ months before)
- [ ] Check thyroid function
- [ ] Assess hormone balance
- [ ] Screen for infections
- [ ] Review vaccine status
- [ ] Track cycles

**Men:**
- [ ] Semen analysis if concerns
- [ ] Optimize antioxidants
- [ ] Avoid heat to testes (hot tubs, laptops)
- [ ] Address any varicocele

---

## KEY NUTRIENTS FOR PREGNANCY

### Essential Prenatal Nutrients

| Nutrient | Preconception/Pregnancy | Why It Matters |
|----------|------------------------|----------------|
| **Folate** | 800-1000mcg (methylfolate preferred) | Neural tube defects prevention |
| **Iron** | 27-45mg (or per need) | Blood volume, fetal development |
| **Iodine** | 150-220mcg | Thyroid, brain development |
| **Choline** | 450-550mg | Brain development (often missing from prenatals!) |
| **DHA** | 300-600mg minimum | Brain and eye development |
| **Vitamin D** | 2000-4000 IU (per levels) | Immune, bone, pregnancy outcomes |
| **B12** | 2.6mcg minimum | Neural development |
| **Zinc** | 11-15mg | Growth, immune, fertility |
| **Calcium** | 1000mg | Bone development |
| **Magnesium** | 350-400mg | Muscle function, blood pressure |

### Often Overlooked Nutrients

**Choline:**
- Critical for brain development and neural tube
- Most prenatals don't contain enough
- Sources: Eggs (yolks!), liver
- Supplement if diet insufficient

**DHA (Omega-3):**
- Essential for fetal brain and eye development
- Most prenatals lack adequate amounts
- Supplement separately: 300-600mg DHA minimum

**Vitamin D:**
- Many women are deficient
- Associated with preeclampsia, gestational diabetes risk
- Test and optimize (40-60 ng/mL for pregnancy)

### Folate vs. Folic Acid

**Folic acid** = Synthetic form (must be converted)
**Folate/Methylfolate** = Active form (preferred)

Up to 40% of women have MTHFR variants that impair folic acid conversion.
**Methylfolate bypasses this issue.**

Choose a prenatal with **methylfolate** (L-5-MTHF), not folic acid.

---

## FERTILITY SUPPORT

### Female Fertility

**Optimize egg quality:**

| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| CoQ10 (Ubiquinol) | 400-600mg | Mitochondrial support for eggs |
| Vitamin D | Optimize 40-60 | Fertility and implantation |
| DHEA | 25-75mg (with guidance) | Ovarian reserve (specific indications) |
| Myo-inositol | 2-4g | PCOS, egg quality |
| NAC | 600mg | Antioxidant, PCOS |
| Omega-3 | 2g | Inflammation, hormone support |
| Vitamin E | 400 IU | Antioxidant |

**Lifestyle:**
- Reduce toxin exposure
- Manage weight (both over and under can affect fertility)
- Reduce stress
- Moderate exercise (not excessive)
- Sleep optimization
- Address thyroid issues

### Male Fertility

**Sperm takes 74 days to develop — Start 3 months before.**

| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| CoQ10 | 200-400mg | Sperm motility, count |
| Zinc | 30mg | Sperm production |
| Selenium | 200mcg | Sperm motility |
| Vitamin C | 500-1000mg | Antioxidant |
| Vitamin E | 400 IU | Antioxidant |
| L-Carnitine | 2-3g | Sperm motility |
| Omega-3 | 2g | Sperm membrane |
| Folate | 400-800mcg | DNA quality |

**Lifestyle:**
- Avoid heat to testes (saunas, hot tubs, laptops on lap)
- Limit alcohol
- No smoking or cannabis
- Reduce environmental toxins
- Optimize weight
- Boxers vs. briefs (some evidence for looser)

---

## DURING PREGNANCY

### First Trimester (Weeks 1-12)

**Critical period for organ development.**

**Focus:**
- Continue prenatal with methylfolate
- Manage nausea (ginger, B6, small frequent meals)
- Avoid toxins (alcohol, smoking, raw fish, certain meds)
- Rest as needed
- Stay hydrated

**Nausea support:**
| Remedy | Dose |
|--------|------|
| Ginger | 1g/day (divided) |
| Vitamin B6 | 10-25mg 3x/day |
| Vitamin B6 + Doxylamine | OTC combo (Unisom + B6) |

### Second Trimester (Weeks 13-26)

**Baby is growing rapidly.**

**Focus:**
- Ensure adequate DHA intake
- Ensure adequate choline
- Monitor iron status (needs increase)
- Continue prenatal
- Gentle exercise
- Monitor blood pressure

### Third Trimester (Weeks 27-40)

**Final growth and preparation.**

**Focus:**
- Adequate protein for growth
- Continue supplements
- Probiotics for GI health and baby's microbiome
- Prepare for postpartum
- Red raspberry leaf tea (last 6 weeks, traditional uterine toner)

**Third trimester additions:**
| Supplement | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| Probiotics | Microbiome for baby, GI health |
| Red raspberry leaf | Uterine tone (last 6 weeks) |
| Dates | Traditional, some evidence for easier labor |

---

## COMMON PREGNANCY CONCERNS

### Gestational Diabetes

**Risk factors:** Overweight, family history, PCOS, previous GD

**Prevention/Management:**
- Blood sugar-balancing diet (low glycemic)
- Adequate protein and healthy fats
- Regular movement
- Chromium 200mcg (if not in prenatal)
- Myo-inositol may help (4g)
- Monitor per OB guidance

### Preeclampsia

**Risk factors:** First pregnancy, history, obesity, multiples, autoimmune

**Potential protective factors:**
- Vitamin D optimization (start preconception)
- Calcium 1-2g if low dietary intake
- Omega-3
- Low-dose aspirin (per OB recommendation)
- Blood pressure monitoring

### Constipation

Common due to progesterone and iron supplements.

**Management:**
- Magnesium citrate or glycinate 300-400mg
- Fiber (gradually increase)
- Hydration
- Movement
- Probiotic
- Consider different iron form if severe

### Heartburn/Reflux

**Management:**
- Small, frequent meals
- Don't lie down after eating
- Elevate head of bed
- Avoid trigger foods
- Ginger tea
- DGL (deglycyrrhizinated licorice) 400mg before meals

---

## SUPPLEMENTS TO AVOID IN PREGNANCY

| Supplement | Reason |
|------------|--------|
| Vitamin A (retinol) >10,000 IU | Teratogenic |
| High-dose vitamin E | May increase bleeding risk |
| Certain herbs | Uterine stimulants (see below) |
| Excess iodine | Thyroid issues |
| Black cohosh | Uterine effects |
| Blue cohosh | Potentially toxic |
| Dong quai | Uterine stimulant |
| Pennyroyal | Toxic |
| High-dose licorice | Preterm risk |
| Goldenseal | Uterine effects |

**When in doubt, check with your provider.**

---

## POSTPARTUM SUPPORT

### Recovery Priorities

1. **Nutrient repletion** — Pregnancy depletes stores
2. **Rest** — Sleep when possible
3. **Nourishment** — Eating well while exhausted is hard
4. **Emotional support** — Watch for postpartum depression/anxiety
5. **Breastfeeding support** — If choosing to breastfeed

### Postpartum Nutrients

| Nutrient | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| Continue prenatal | Repletion |
| Iron | If lost blood, rebuild |
| Omega-3 | Mood, milk |
| Vitamin D | Immune, mood |
| B vitamins | Energy |
| Magnesium | Recovery, sleep |

### Postpartum Depression/Anxiety

**Risk factors:** History of depression, traumatic birth, lack of support, hormonal sensitivity

**Supportive approach:**
- Omega-3 (high EPA) 2-3g
- Vitamin D optimization
- B vitamins
- Magnesium
- Sleep (when possible)
- Social support
- Professional help if needed

**Seek help if:**
- Persistent sadness or anxiety
- Difficulty bonding with baby
- Intrusive thoughts
- Unable to sleep even when baby sleeps
- Feeling hopeless

---

## BREASTFEEDING

### Nutrients Passed Through Milk

**What you eat affects milk composition:**
- Omega-3 (DHA) — Directly reflects intake
- Vitamin D — Supplementation increases milk levels
- B vitamins — Reflects status
- Iodine — Important for baby's thyroid

### Lactation Support

**Galactagogues (increase milk supply):**
| Option | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| Fenugreek | Most common, can cause maple syrup smell |
| Blessed thistle | Often combined with fenugreek |
| Fennel | Gentle |
| Oats | Food form |
| Brewer's yeast | Often in lactation cookies |
| Moringa | Emerging evidence |

**Also important:**
- Adequate hydration
- Adequate calories (breastfeeding burns 300-500 cal/day)
- Rest (affects supply)
- Frequent nursing/pumping
- Lactation consultant support

### What to Avoid While Breastfeeding

- Excess caffeine (limit to 200-300mg)
- Alcohol (occasional small amount, timing matters)
- Certain medications (check with provider)
- Most supplements are okay but verify

---

 === SECTION 2: PATIENT COMMUNICATION [PATIENT_OK] ===
## PRECONCEPTION OPTIMIZATION PROTOCOL

### Female Protocol (3-6 Months Before TTC)

**Assessment:**
| Test | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| CBC | Anemia, iron status |
| Ferritin | Iron stores |
| Vitamin D | Optimize |
| TSH, Free T4, TPO | Thyroid |
| Fasting glucose, insulin | Metabolic |
| AMH (if fertility concern) | Ovarian reserve |
| Hormone panel (Day 3 + Day 21) | Hormone balance |
| Rubella, Varicella titers | Immunity |
| Infectious disease screen | STIs, etc. |

**Supplement Protocol:**
| Supplement | Dose | Notes |
|------------|------|-------|
| Prenatal (methylfolate-based) | Per product | Start now |
| DHA | 300-600mg | Separate from prenatal |
| Vitamin D | Per levels | Target 40-60 ng/mL |
| CoQ10 (Ubiquinol) | 400-600mg | Egg quality |
| Iron | Per status | If low |
| Choline | 450mg | If not in prenatal |
| Probiotic | Multi-strain | Gut health |

**Lifestyle:**
- Anti-inflammatory diet
- Reduce alcohol, caffeine
- No smoking
- Toxin reduction (clean products, avoid plastics)
- Stress management
- Sleep optimization
- Moderate exercise

### Male Protocol (3 Months Before TTC)

**Assessment:**
| Test | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| Semen analysis | Baseline (if any concerns) |
| Testosterone, FSH, LH | Hormone status (if needed) |
| Vitamin D | Optimize |
| Basic metabolic | General health |

**Supplement Protocol:**
| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| CoQ10 | 200-400mg | Sperm quality |
| Zinc | 30mg | Sperm production |
| Selenium | 200mcg | Sperm motility |
| Vitamin C | 500mg | Antioxidant |
| Vitamin E | 400 IU | Antioxidant |
| L-Carnitine | 2g | Sperm motility |
| Omega-3 | 2g | Sperm membrane |
| Folate | 400mcg | DNA quality |

**Lifestyle:**
- Avoid heat (hot tubs, laptops on lap)
- No smoking, limit alcohol
- Reduce toxins
- Exercise moderately
- Maintain healthy weight
- Manage stress

### PCOS Fertility Protocol

**See 18_FEMALE_HORMONES for full PCOS protocol**

**Fertility-focused additions:**
| Supplement | Dose | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| Myo-inositol | 2-4g | Egg quality, ovulation |
| D-chiro-inositol | 50-100mg | Combined with myo |
| CoQ10 | 400mg | Egg quality |
| NAC | 600mg | Antioxidant, insulin |
| Vitamin D | Optimize | Ovulation support |
| Omega-3 | 2g | Inflammation |
| Berberine | 500mg 2x | Insulin sensitization |

## PREGNANCY PROTOCOLS

### Comprehensive Pregnancy Supplement Protocol

**Prenatal base (ensure contains):**
| Nutrient | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Methylfolate | 800-1000mcg |
| Iron | 27mg (may need more) |
| Iodine | 150-220mcg |
| B12 | 2.6mcg+ |
| Zinc | 11-15mg |
| Calcium | 200-300mg (diet provides rest) |

**Add separately:**
| Supplement | Dose | Trimester |
|------------|------|-----------|
| DHA | 300-600mg | All |
| Vitamin D | 2000-4000 IU (per levels) | All |
| Choline | 450mg (if not in prenatal) | All |
| Magnesium | 300-400mg | All |
| Probiotic | Multi-strain | All, especially 3rd |

### Gestational Diabetes Support

**Diet:**
- Low glycemic index/load
- Protein at every meal
- Limit refined carbs
- Regular small meals
- Post-meal walking

**Supplements:**
| Supplement | Dose | Notes |
|------------|------|-------|
| Myo-inositol | 2-4g | May improve insulin sensitivity |
| Chromium | 200mcg | If not in prenatal |
| Magnesium | 400mg | Glucose metabolism |
| Vitamin D | Optimize | Insulin sensitivity |

**Monitor per OB guidelines.**

### Preeclampsia Prevention

**For at-risk women:**
| Intervention | Dose | Notes |
|--------------|------|-------|
| Low-dose aspirin | 81-150mg | Per OB (usually start 12-16 weeks) |
| Calcium | 1-2g | If low dietary intake |
| Vitamin D | Optimize | Start preconception |
| Omega-3 | 2g | Anti-inflammatory |
| Magnesium | 400mg | Blood pressure |

**Monitor blood pressure, proteinuria per OB.**

### Postpartum Depression Prevention/Support

**Risk assessment:**
- History of depression
- Previous PPD
- Stressful life events
- Lack of support
- Traumatic birth

**Protocol:**
| Supplement | Dose | Notes |
|------------|------|-------|
| Omega-3 (high EPA) | 2-3g EPA | Start 3rd trimester, continue |
| Vitamin D | Optimize | Mood support |
| B-complex | Per product | Energy, neurotransmitters |
| Magnesium | 400mg | Sleep, mood |
| Probiotic | Multi-strain | Gut-brain |

**Support:**
- Social support arrangements
- Sleep planning
- Professional support ready if needed
- Screen with Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale

---

 === SECTION 3: PHARMACOKINETICS & DYNAMICS (ADME) [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## PRECONCEPTION TIMELINE

**3-6 months before:**
- Start prenatal (methylfolate-based)
- Optimize vitamin D
- Add CoQ10 for egg quality
- Clean up diet and lifestyle
- Address any health issues

## CRITICAL PREGNANCY NUTRIENTS

| Nutrient | Dose |
|----------|------|
| Methylfolate | 800-1000mcg |
| DHA | 300-600mg |
| Vitamin D | 2000-4000 IU |
| Choline | 450mg |
| Iron | 27mg+ |
| Iodine | 150-220mcg |

**Most prenatals lack adequate DHA and choline — supplement separately!**

## FERTILITY SUPPORT

**Female:** CoQ10 400-600mg, Vitamin D, Omega-3
**Male:** CoQ10, Zinc 30mg, L-Carnitine, Selenium

## WHAT TO AVOID

- Vitamin A (retinol) >10,000 IU
- Black cohosh, blue cohosh
- High-dose herbs
- Alcohol, smoking
- Certain medications (check with provider)

## POSTPARTUM PRIORITIES

1. Continue prenatal
2. Omega-3 for mood
3. Vitamin D
4. Sleep (when possible)
5. Support system
6. Watch for PPD signs

## BREASTFEEDING SUPPLY

- Fenugreek
- Blessed thistle
- Adequate hydration
- Adequate calories
- Rest
- Frequent nursing

---
